final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial photo of a primeval forest at dawn, with Tyndall light beams, ancient moss-covered rocks, and a winding mountain stream.

Style/medium: photorealistic large-format nature photography, wide-angle lens, in the spirit of Art Wolfe or Frans Lanting wilderness editorial work
Composition: wide-angle shot with pronounced depth of field — foreground ferns and wild mushrooms, mid-ground towering redwood trunks, background hazy mountain ridgeline silhouette; layered three-plane depth
Subject: ancient moss-covered boulders bathed in Tyndall light shafts piercing a dense forest canopy; a clear winding mountain stream in the mid-distance with reflections of lush green vegetation on its surface
Scene/backdrop: deep primeval forest interior at first light of dawn; thin mist drifting between trees; distant mountain contours faintly visible through the canopy
Lighting/mood: first rays of morning sun breaking through a thick overhead canopy, forming distinct Tyndall-effect light columns; rich layered chiaroscuro; mysterious yet serene atmosphere
Color palette: deep forest green and emerald green as dominant tones, warm gold as accent where light strikes, cool shadow tones in the understory — all three in deliberate contrast
Materials/textures: velvety moss on rough stone surfaces, wet bark grain on redwood trunks, translucent fern fronds, glistening water surface with soft reflections
Avoid: artificial HDR halos, oversaturated green, plastic foliage, lens flare overload, watermark, 8K stuffer aesthetic, flat lighting
